staying_up_late: Tonight: Move your phone 30cm from your face. Turn brightness to minimum. Take 5 deep breaths. Your brain will switch to 'sleep prep' mode.
Late: Staying awake past 1 AM causes measurable damage to your brain and heart. What you're doing is 100x less important than sleep. Go to bed now.
Wrap up: The blue light is telling your brain it's noon. Your body is confused. Help it by putting the screen away.
Midnight: Move your phone 30cm from your face. Turn brightness to minimum. Take 5 deep breaths. Your brain will switch to 'sleep prep' mode.
1 AM: One hour tonight ruins 8 hours tomorrow. Get in bed now.
Tonight: A sleep-deprived brain has the same judgment as 0.1% blood alcohol. 17 hours awake = legally drunk. Protect tomorrow's decisions by sleeping tonight.
Late: Your willpower is at its lowest right now. Don't fight the urge to scroll—just remove the phone. Put it in another room.
Wrap up: One hour of sleep tonight = 3 hours of productivity tomorrow. The math is simple. Go to bed.
Midnight: A sleep-deprived brain has the same judgment as 0.1% blood alcohol. 17 hours awake = legally drunk. Protect tomorrow's decisions by sleeping tonight.
1 AM: Blue light tells your brain it's daytime. Flip your phone and close your eyes.
Tonight: Will you remember this content tomorrow at noon? But sleep deprivation will definitely hurt your focus, judgment, and immunity. Which matters more?
Late: Imagine waking up refreshed vs. waking up exhausted. Which version of tomorrow do you want? You decide right now.
Wrap up: One hour tonight ruins 8 hours tomorrow. Get in bed now.
